summary: Sweden's transition is steered by the National Cyber Security Centre, hosted by the National Defence Radio Establishment (FRA), which has issued national recommendations for how organisations should carry out the move to quantum-safe cryptography. The recommendations are framed in relation to NIS2 and the Swedish cybersecurity act, and Parliament has welcomed the European approach to promoting the transition.
govActors:
NCSC Sweden | National Cyber Security Centre, issues national recommendations for the transition to quantum-safe cryptography
FRA | National Defence Radio Establishment, hosts the NCSC and presents on the transition
Riksdagen | Swedish Parliament, fact memos welcoming the EU quantum strategy and digital-infrastructure approach
Government of Sweden | identifies the transition to quantum-safe cryptography as part of strengthening Europe's cybersecurity
